name: crates.io API
what_it_does: Official Rust package registry REST API for searching, downloading, and publishing crates (Rust packages), used by Cargo (Rust's package manager) under the hood.
use_cases: Searching crates.io for Rust packages by keyword, category, or author
Fetching crate metadata including versions, downloads, and dependencies
Checking for security advisories or yanked versions before dependency selection
Automating crate publishing in Rust project CI/CD pipelines
Building Rust dependency analysis and security audit tools
not_for: Non-Rust package management (use npm, PyPI, or Maven instead)
Private package hosting (use Artifactory or Cloudsmith instead)
best_when: An agent needs to discover, analyze, or manage Rust packages and dependencies as part of a Rust development workflow.
avoid_when: You need private crate hosting or are working with non-Rust languages.

auth.notes: API tokens from crates.io account settings with scope selection (publish-new, publish-update, yank, etc.). Read operations (search, metadata) are unauthenticated. Publish/yank requires scoped token.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; operated by the Rust Foundation
pricing.notes: Operated by the Rust Foundation as a public good. Rate limits apply to prevent abuse.

performance.rate_limits: 1 request/second for unauthenticated; 10 requests/second for authenticated

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is well-maintained and accurate. Error responses are JSON with descriptive messages. Rate limit information is documented and enforced via response headers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are naturally idempotent. Publish operations are version-gated — you cannot publish the same version twice.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit is 1 req/s for anonymous and 10 req/s for authenticated — bulk scanning must throttle
User-Agent header is required; crates.io blocks requests without a proper User-Agent
Some data (full feature flags, dev dependencies) only available in downloaded crate metadata, not API
Yanked crates are still downloadable but flagged — check yank status in dependency resolution

security_score_components.security_notes: Scoped API tokens with least-privilege model (publish-new, yank, etc.). Open-source registry code (auditable). cargo-audit integration provides supply chain security. Rust Foundation governance.
